Caption: Vous utilisez ChatGPT pour votre activité immobilière ? Alors voici l’erreur que beaucoup de professionnels commettent encore. Ils demandent simplement : "Rédige-moi une annonce immobilière." ou "Fais-moi un post LinkedIn." Résultat : un texte générique, peu différenciant et rarement adapté à leur cible. L’IA n’est pas magique. La qualité du résultat dépend directement de la qualité de la demande. Par exemple, au lieu de demander : ❌ "Rédige une annonce immobilière." Essayez : ✅ "Rédige une annonce immobilière pour une maison familiale de 120 m² avec jardin, située dans un secteur recherché. Le ton doit être rassurant, professionnel et mettre en avant la qualité de vie du quartier." La différence est immédiate. L’enjeu aujourd’hui n’est pas seulement de savoir utiliser l’IA. C’est surtout de savoir lui donner les bonnes consignes pour obtenir des contenus réellement utiles à votre activité. Chez Les Formateurs Immo, nous accompagnons les professionnels de l’immobilier à intégrer ces nouveaux outils avec une approche concrète et directement applicable sur le terrain. Et vous, quelle est la tâche que vous aimeriez automatiser ou améliorer grâce à l’IA ? IAImmobilier ChatGPT FormationImmobilière

Caption: 🚀💻 **Aprendizaje Inteligente en Programación: Evidencia Científica para la Educación del Futuro** La Inteligencia Artificial está transformando la enseñanza de la programación. Este estudio presenta una revisión sistemática de la literatura que analiza cómo las tecnologías inteligentes están mejorando el aprendizaje de la programación en distintos niveles educativos. 🔍 **Hallazgos principales:** ✔️ **Problema abordado:** La enseñanza de la programación enfrenta desafíos relacionados con la personalización del aprendizaje, la retroalimentación oportuna y el desarrollo de habilidades de resolución de problemas. Las tecnologías basadas en IA surgen como una alternativa prometedora para enfrentar estas limitaciones. ✔️ **Metodología rigurosa:** Mediante el protocolo PRISMA se analizaron **125 estudios científicos** provenientes de **Scopus** e **IEEE Xplore**, seleccionando finalmente **22 investigaciones de alta calidad** publicadas entre 2022 y 2026. ✔️ **Resultados destacados:** 🤖 Los **Sistemas Tutores Inteligentes (ITS)**, los **Modelos de Lenguaje de Gran Escala (LLMs)** como ChatGPT, las plataformas de aprendizaje adaptativo y los entornos gamificados fueron las tecnologías más utilizadas. 📈 Los estudios reportan mejoras significativas en: • Rendimiento académico • Resolución de problemas • Depuración de código • Pensamiento computacional • Motivación y compromiso estudiantil ⚠️ **Desafíos identificados:** Persisten preocupaciones relacionadas con la dependencia excesiva de herramientas de IA, respuestas incorrectas generadas por los modelos, sesgos algorítmicos y la escasez de estudios de largo plazo. 💡 **Conclusión principal:** La Inteligencia Artificial tiene el potencial de revolucionar la educación en programación, pero sus mejores resultados se alcanzan cuando complementa la labor docente y favorece experiencias de aprendizaje más personalizadas, adaptativas y centradas en el estudiante. 📚 Revisión Sistemática de Literatura 🎓 Educación en Programación • Inteligencia Artificial • ChatGPT • Sistemas Tutores Inteligentes • Aprendizaje Adaptativo InteligenciaArtificial Educacion Programacion ChatGPT LLM SmartLearning TecnologiaEduca

Caption: Prebuilt AI is closing the gap between "enterprise only" and "actually accessible." MaiAgent just debuted at VivaTech 2026 making exactly this case: stop building your AI stack from scratch. Caption: GenUI shows the result. But where does the agent get the data? 🔌 Meet MCP — the Model Context Protocol. The clean mental model: MCP fetches and acts; GenUI builds the interface on top. Agent calls a tool (search_flights) → gets structured JSON → emits an A2UI blueprint (flight cards + Select) → your native app renders it → tap Select → book_flight → loop. Every action is an MCP call; every screen is a GenUI surface. Why now: donated to the Linux Foundation (Dec 2025), ~10k public servers, early-majority adoption. Caption: Your store is closed. The sale isn't. Meta just rolled out Business Agent at Cannes Lions: an AI that replies, recommends products and closes the sale 24/7 inside WhatsApp, Instagram and Messenger DMs. Over 1M businesses are already running it. The shift is real: the highest-intent moment is a customer sliding into your DMs at midnight, and most businesses miss it because nobody is awake to answer. An always-on agent catches that intent, answers the question, and sends the checkout link before the buyer cools off. How to use it without losing your brand voice: 1. Feed it your real catalogue, prices, shipping and returns so answers are accurate. 2. Set the tone and the hard rules (what it can discount, when to hand off to a human). 3. Give it one job per conversation: answer, recommend, then close with a link. 4. Review the transcripts weekly and tighten the gaps. Treat the agent as a junior closer you train, not a black box you trust blindly. Would you let an AI close sales in your DMs? Caption: Insurance claims are not just an operations process. They are where trust is won or lost. When claims move through emails, spreadsheets, old portals, and manual reviews, the business creates hidden risk. That risk shows up as: • slower decisions • poor customer updates • duplicate work • missed recovery opportunities • overpayment • higher support volume • lower retention This is called claims leakage. And it is one of the quietest profit drains in insurance. PwC found that improving claims leakage can often create 5–10% savings. JD Power also found that customers who rate their digital claims experience as poor or just okay are far more likely to leave or not renew. The lesson is simple: Claims modernization is not just about speed. It is about better data, cleaner workflows, smarter routing, clearer updates, and stronger trust. AI can help. Automation can help. Better SaaS platforms can help. But only when the system is built around real insurance workflows. At Zenveus, we help insurance companies build custom software, AI workflows, SaaS platforms, customer portals, and automation systems that make operations faster, cleaner, and easier to scale. Caption: Most IAM reviews focus on individual permissions. But attackers don't think that way. They look for combinations. A permission that appears harmless on its own can become dangerous when combined with another permission elsewhere in the environment. Consider examples like: • KMS Decrypt + S3 Read • Secrets Manager Access + Lambda Update • CloudTrail Stop Logging + IAM Modification • S3 Policy Changes + Bucket Read/Write Individually, these permissions may not trigger major concern. Together, they can create a direct path to data exposure, privilege escalation, or reduced visibility during an attack. This is one of the biggest challenges in cloud security today. Most tools identify individual findings. Few analyze how permissions interact across identities, resources, and services to create hidden attack paths. Securitain was built to address exactly this problem. By analyzing IAM permissions, trust relationships, resource access, and toxic permission combinations, Securitain helps AWS teams uncover risks that may remain hidden in traditional security reviews. Because the real risk is often not a single permission. It's the combination. 🔐 Detect hidden attack paths 🔐 Identify toxic permission combinations 🔐 Prioritize risks based on real impact 🔐 Improve AWS IAM security posture Securitain by Cloudain Making cloud security findings easier to understand, prioritize, and remediate. AWS CloudSecurity IAM CyberSecurity DevSecOps SecurityPosture LeastPrivilege AWSIAM Securitain Cloudain

Caption: Toda operação de TI tem pontos de atenção. O problema é quando eles só aparecem depois que já geraram impacto. Um acesso mal configurado, uma permissão excessiva, uma falha de segurança ou um recurso exposto podem passar despercebidos na rotina. E, quando a visibilidade depende apenas de análise manual, o time acaba trabalhando mais para corrigir urgências do que para prevenir problemas. O Fortis foi criado para mudar esse cenário. Caption: Innovation Or Better Marketing? A new AI infrastructure battle is heating up. Databricks recently unveiled its LTAP architecture, calling it the first system to combine transactional and analytical databases into a single copy. SingleStore wasn't impressed. CEO Raj Verma quickly pushed back, arguing that SingleStore has offered the same capability for years and already serves hundreds of customers using it. The company's response was blunt: "We didn't announce it. We shipped it, seven years ago." SingleStore also challenged Databricks' open-source narrative, claiming the architecture creates deeper customer lock-in. Here's what most people miss: In tech, winning isn't always about inventing first. It's often about owning the narrative. Many breakthrough technologies become famous only when a larger player enters the market. Real question: What matters more in technology: being first to build it, or being the company that convinces the world it matters? GenLeaders Databricks SingleStore AIInfrastructure DataInfrastructure

Caption: $6.9 billion in annualized revenue and it’s actually speeding up. Databricks just announced its annualized revenue hit $6.9B, up more than 80% year-over-year from $5.4B last quarter. CEO Ali Ghodsi shared the milestone at the company’s Data and AI Summit. The AI product line alone now runs at $1.7B, up from $1.4B just four months earlier, with net revenue retention holding above 140%. But there’s a twist: gross margins slipped to ~74% from above 80% as AI agents fire off far more queries and compute, and Ghodsi expects them to fall further. At a $134B valuation, Databricks is now worth more than public rival Snowflake. Is shrinking margins the real cost of the agentic AI boom? 👇 Databricks AI enterpriseAI dataanalytics AliGhodsi AIagents

Caption: "AGI is here today. Just not at work." Caption: This is the SQL window function cheat sheet I wish people memorized. Not every function is a random trick. They fall into groups. Number rows: ROW_NUMBER, RANK, DENSE_RANK. Look around: LAG, LEAD. Calculate without collapsing: SUM, AVG, COUNT, MIN, MAX. Control the window: PARTITION BY, ORDER BY, and frames. That is the real interview pattern. If you can say which group, which order, and which nearby rows matter, most window function questions become simple. Save this before your next SQL interview or analytics PR. Which one still trips you up? HackProduct SQL DataEngineering AnalyticsEngineering InterviewPrep

Caption: ¿Tu negocio crea valor con sus datos o solo quema dinero? 📊🔥 La mayoría de las empresas no sufren por falta de datos. Tienen ventas, clientes, inventario y marketing registrándose 24/7. El verdadero problema es la falta de dirección. He visto demasiados equipos invertir miles de dólares en dashboards complejos, reportes infinitos y herramientas de analítica que al final nadie usa para tomar decisiones. Datos que se acumulan en un data lake o en hojas de cálculo, pero que jamás se traducen en acción. Eso no es gestión de datos. Eso es gasto de datos. 💸 Y ojo, esto se vuelve crítico si tienes en la mira la automatización o la Inteligencia Artificial. El 90% de los proyectos de IA y Machine Learning fracasan por lo mismo: no tener una infraestructura de datos sólida detrás. Sin esa base estructurada, ningún algoritmo (por más avanzado o costoso que sea) te va a dar resultados confiables. Basura entra, basura sale. Un negocio que realmente sabe monetizar y aprovechar su información, responde estas 3 preguntas antes de tocar una sola línea de código o comprar un software: 1️⃣ ¿Qué decisión de negocio quiero mejorar con esta información? 2️⃣ ¿Quién la va a usar en el día a día y para qué exactamente? 3️⃣ ¿Cómo voy a medir el impacto financiero ocn esa decisión? Sin estas respuestas claras, cualquier inversión en infraestructura o analítica es solo ruido caro. 👉 Desliza en el carrusel para ver la diferencia exacta entre los datos que generan ROI y los que solo generan costos en la nube, y los pasos clave para empezar a ordenar tu casa digital. 📩 ¿Quieres auditar cómo están tus datos hoy y dónde está la oportunidad real de optimización en tu negocio? Caption: Two months in. Here is what building with AI actually looks like for one of our engineers. The technical work was real from week one. A Snowflake MCP Gateway deployed on Azure. A product query agent for a retail client where the debugging never really stopped. An order processing pipeline with human review queues, business rule validation, and a conversational interface operators could use in plain language. Then came the work she did not expect. Co-facilitating AI training sessions for enterprise teams. Building a product-specific agent. Now moving into a finance agent where the data is more sensitive, the rules are stricter, and the margin for error is smaller. The lesson that stuck. The model is almost never where it breaks. It breaks at the context. The schema. The structure of what you hand the system before it is asked to do anything. Pratiksha came in from a marketing analytics background. It turned out to be the thing that made her useful fastest. Knowing what a business team actually needs from a tool is not a soft skill in this work. It is the job. Building a system well and explaining it well are completely different skills. Follow to hear more such stories. Topics: AI engineering, production AI, AI agents, enterprise AI

Caption: Trust is usually treated like a brand issue, but in modern marketing it is also tied closely to how well the business is organized behind the scenes. A company can have a good logo, a polished website, and strong visuals, but still create a weak customer experience if the systems behind the marketing are disconnected. You see this when an ad sends people to a page that does not match the offer, when an inquiry form does not trigger the right follow-up, when customer information sits in the wrong place, or when the sales conversation feels disconnected from what the marketing promised. Customers may not see the system behind the business, but they feel the result of it. They feel it when the response is slow, when the next step is unclear, when the message changes from one place to another, or when the business does not feel as organized after the inquiry as it did before. That is why marketing infrastructure matters. The website, CRM, email follow-up, lead handling, advertising, content, automation, and reporting should work together as one connected customer journey. When those pieces are aligned, the business has more control over what happens after someone shows interest. This is where modern marketing systems become valuable. They do not just help a business move faster. They help the business respond more consistently, manage leads more properly, and create a stronger experience from first impression to follow-up. For businesses that want to grow, the goal is not just to generate more attention. The goal is to make sure the system behind the attention is strong enough to support the customer when they are ready to move forward. Caption: Digital Marketing (SEO, PPC & Lead Generation) A business owner told me last year that digital marketing does not work for his industry. I asked him what he had tried. He ran a few Facebook ads two years ago. Got some clicks. No conversions. Stopped. That was his entire data set. One short experiment, written off as proof. Meanwhile his direct competitor, same city, same industry, had spent eighteen months building a content and SEO strategy that now brings in 60% of their revenue from inbound leads. They did not get lucky. They got consistent. Here is what the data actually says about businesses that invest properly in digital marketing. SEO leads have a 14.6% close rate. Outbound leads average 1.7%. The same sales effort closes at nearly nine times the rate when the prospect found you first instead of the other way around. 70% of B2B buyers have already completed their research before they ever contact a vendor. If your business is not visible during that research phase, you do not lose the deal. You never enter the consideration set at all. Businesses that maintain a consistent content strategy generate 67% more leads per month than those that do not. Not from increasing ad spend. From expertise that works while they sleep. The businesses I see winning are rarely the ones with the single best product in their category. They are the ones prospects find first, trust fastest, and remember longest. Marketing is not a cost center sitting separately from the business. It is the mechanism that makes every other part of the business possible to sell. The founder who gave up after one failed ad campaign was not wrong that his first attempt did not work. He was wrong to conclude that meant the channel did not work. The question worth asking is not whether you can afford to invest properly in digital marketing. It is what it is quietly costing you, every single month, that you do not. DigitalMarketing SEO LeadGeneration PPC BusinessGrowth

Caption: Ich glaube, wir haben vergessen, dass Reichweite nicht nur ein Privileg ist. Sondern Verantwortung. In einer Zeit, in der wir mit einem einzigen Post Millionen Menschen erreichen können, reicht es für mich nicht mehr, nur schön auszusehen, schön zu wohnen, Produkte zu verkaufen oder dem nächsten Trend hinterherzulaufen. Denn jede Empfehlung ist eine Entscheidung. Jede Kooperation ist eine Entscheidung. Jedes Schweigen ist eine Entscheidung. Und ich glaube, wir dürfen uns öfter fragen: Würde ich diese Marke auch unterstützen, wenn niemand dafür bezahlen würde? Passt das zu meinen Werten? Mache ich die Welt damit ein kleines bisschen besser oder einfach nur lauter? Für mich bedeutet Social Media zu machen nicht, Menschen zum Kaufen zu bringen. Es bedeutet: Menschen zum Denken zu bringen. Zum Fühlen. Zum Hinterfragen. Zum Verbinden. Ich möchte keine Marken unterstützen, die Menschen ausbeuten. Ich möchte keine Kampagnen machen, für die ich meine Werte an der Garderobe abgebe. Ich möchte meine Stimme nutzen für mentale Gesundheit, Gleichberechtigung, Nachhaltigkeit, Inklusion und die Themen, die mir wirklich wichtig sind. Und nein, ich mache das nicht perfekt. Aber ich versuche es bewusst. Denn wir leben in einer Welt, die uns permanent sagt: Mehr kaufen. Mehr besitzen. Mehr optimieren. Während wir eigentlich viel mehr brauchen: Mehr Menschlichkeit. Mehr Gemeinschaft. Mehr Verantwortung. Vielleicht ist das die eigentliche Frage: Nicht, wie groß unsere Reichweite ist. Sondern wofür wir sie nutzen. 🤍 contentcreator influencermarketing mentalhealth nachhaltigkeit feminismus
Author: laurahollmer_

Caption: 🎙️ La Skwatt Box sur ! Nous avons eu le plaisir d'être invités à participer à l'émission "La France Bouge", animée par , dans les locaux de La Poste Pro à Strasbourg, pour présenter Skwatt et notre vision de la recharge des véhicules électriques. Avec l'essor des véhicules électriques, chaque bâtiment relié au réseau électrique devient potentiellement un point de recharge. Pourtant, le véritable défi n'est pas seulement de fournir de l'énergie, mais aussi de pouvoir contrôler, sécuriser et facturer simplement les recharges. C'est précisément la mission de Skwatt : proposer une alternative légère, économique et fabriquée en France aux bornes de recharge traditionnelles, spécialement pensée pour les gîtes, hôtels, campings, chambres d'hôtes et locations touristiques. Merci à Elisabeth Assayag, aux équipes d'Europe 1 et de La Poste Pro pour leur accueil et cette belle opportunité de mettre en lumière l'innovation française au service de la mobilité électrique. 🚗⚡🇫🇷 Europe1 LaFranceBouge ElisabethAssayag Innovation Startup MobilitéÉlectrique RechargeVE MadeInFrance Skwatt

Caption: Being an employee was never really my thing. For years I’ve been trying to build something of my own, different attempts, different paths, different failures and successes. But things don’t always go the way we want. So I reached a point where I had to start a job again. I like the experience it brings, but the conditions? Not working for me at all. Shifts during weekends for a person like me who believes in work-life balance, who is living away from her hometown and is genuinely alive through her connections and friends, losing the chance of seeing any human being in person during a whole week was far away from my thoughts and expectations. Also the salary sucks and there is no perk and leave at all! But this is the fact of my life right now. I am not complaining. I am sharing this because I know I am not alone in this. Many people, especially founders, are living this double life. Building something meaningful on the side while paying their bills somewhere else. So don’t feel bad or embarrassed if you are experiencing the same, and don’t be surprised when you see a founder with an “Open to Work” badge on their LinkedIn profile. That’s not failure. That’s just the part nobody posts about. I chose this path consciously. Not because I had no options, but because building something meaningful takes time, and time needs stability. The job is the bridge. What I’m building is the destination. If you’re somewhere in this same messy in-between, building on the side, figuring it out, holding two lives at once, my DM is open. You don’t have to figure it out alone. solofounder solopreneur enterpreneur startup

Caption: Insurance claims are not just an operations process. They are where trust is won or lost. When claims move through emails, spreadsheets, old portals, and manual reviews, the business creates hidden risk. That risk shows up as: • slower decisions • poor customer updates • duplicate work • missed recovery opportunities • overpayment • higher support volume • lower retention This is called claims leakage. And it is one of the quietest profit drains in insurance. PwC found that improving claims leakage can often create 5–10% savings. JD Power also found that customers who rate their digital claims experience as poor or just okay are far more likely to leave or not renew. The lesson is simple: Claims modernization is not just about speed. It is about better data, cleaner workflows, smarter routing, clearer updates, and stronger trust. AI can help. Automation can help. Better SaaS platforms can help. But only when the system is built around real insurance workflows. At Zenveus, we help insurance companies build custom software, AI workflows, SaaS platforms, customer portals, and automation systems that make operations faster, cleaner, and easier to scale. The future of insurance will belong to companies that make claims feel simple. insurance insurtech insurancetechnology claimsautomation aiininsurance insurancesoftware saas digitaltransformation insuranceclaims automation customsoftwaredevelopment zenveus
